worth_it_explanation: Thought Leader Ads put a person's name and face on a sponsored post instead of the company page, and members read a post from a named expert differently from a brand ad. The checks here are quick to run inside Campaign Manager. You confirm whether the format is being used where a credible voice exists, and whether any Thought Leader Ad already built sits under an objective the format actually supports. Both are configuration observations, not rebuilds, so a finding turns into a short change rather than a project.
intro: Thought Leader Ads promote a post from a person, such as a founder or a subject-matter expert, rather than a post from the company page. The format requires the member's permission and is available for a limited set of objectives. These checks confirm whether the account uses the format where a credible voice exists, and whether any Thought Leader Ad in the account is paired with an objective the format supports.

audit_checks.title: Thought Leader Ads are not used to amplify employee or executive voice
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When every sponsored post runs from the company page, the account never tests whether a named person's post earns more engagement for the same spend. The format requires only the member's permission to enable, so leaving it unused where a credible voice exists forgoes a test that costs little to set up.

educate.base:
A Thought Leader Ad promotes an organic post published by a person rather than by the company page. The named member can be an employee, an executive, or a connection of the account, and the ad carries that person's name, photo, and profile alongside the post. Members engage with a post from a person they can identify differently from a brand post, so the format gives the account a way to put a credible individual voice behind paid distribution.

The format depends on permission. The account requests the right to sponsor a specific post, and the member approves or declines that request before the post can run as an ad. Without an approved request, there is no Thought Leader Ad to run, so the format stays unused even when a strong post exists.

An account that runs only company-page Sponsored Content never finds out whether a named expert's post would perform better for the same budget. You may want to confirm whether the account has a credible internal voice, a founder, a practitioner, or a customer-facing expert, whose posts could be sponsored, and whether any of those posts have been tested as Thought Leader Ads.

A few signs point to the format being unused:
  • Every active Sponsored Content ad runs from the company page rather than from a named member
  • No sponsorship requests have been sent to members in the account's history
  • Employees or executives publish strong organic posts that have never been promoted
Where a credible voice exists, you may want to consider testing a Thought Leader Ad against the company-page equivalent to see whether the named voice earns more engagement.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business (B2B) store, a Thought Leader Ad can put a founder or a product expert behind a post about how the product is used, which reads as more credible than a company-page promotion. You may want to confirm whether such a voice exists on the team and whether any of those posts have been sponsored.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-capture account, a named expert's post often draws more engagement and profile interest than a company-page ad, which can feed the audiences and remarketing the account uses for lead generation. You may want to confirm whether the account has tested an employee or executive post as a Thought Leader Ad alongside its standard Sponsored Content.

learn_more.source_title: Create thought leader ads in Campaign Manager

investigate.intro: In Campaign Manager, review the active Sponsored Content to confirm whether any ads run from a named member rather than the company page, and whether the account has the voice and the posts to test the format.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager and select the ad account, then open 'Advertise' to view the campaigns and their ad sets.
Open the ads within each Sponsored Content ad set and note whether each ad runs from the company page or from a named member's post.
Open the account's content sources, such as the Content Library or the list of sponsored member posts, and note whether any sponsorship requests have been sent to members.
Note any employee or executive whose organic posts could be sponsored but have not been, so the finding can point to a specific voice the account could test.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether any active Sponsored Content ad runs as a Thought Leader Ad from a named member, and whether a credible internal voice with promotable posts exists. An account that already runs Thought Leader Ads, or that has no credible member voice to sponsor, passes. An account that runs only company-page ads while a credible voice and strong organic posts exist is recorded as an opportunity to test the format.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether any active ad runs as a Thought Leader Ad and whether a credible member voice exists in the account above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} runs its Sponsored Content only from the company page and has not tested Thought Leader Ads, which promote a post from a named member such as a founder or a subject-matter expert. Members engage with a post from a person they can identify differently from a brand post, so the account has not found out whether a credible individual voice would earn more engagement for the same spend. {{voice_recommend}} identifying an employee or executive with strong organic posts, requesting permission to sponsor one of those posts, and running it as a Thought Leader Ad against the company-page equivalent to compare the engagement.

audit_checks.title: Thought Leader Ads are paired with an objective they do not support
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Thought Leader Ads are available only for a limited set of objectives. An ad set built under an unsupported objective cannot run the format, so the setup effort is wasted and the intended Thought Leader Ad never launches under that campaign.

educate.base:
When an account builds a Thought Leader Ad, the campaign objective decides whether the format is even available. LinkedIn offers Thought Leader Ads for a limited set of objectives, such as brand awareness, engagement, and video views, rather than for every objective in Campaign Manager. An ad set built under an objective outside that set cannot carry a Thought Leader Ad, so the format does not appear as an option when the practitioner reaches the creative step.

The mismatch surfaces as wasted setup. The practitioner picks an objective such as lead generation or website conversions, builds the ad set, and then finds that the member-post format is not selectable. The work to that point does not produce a running Thought Leader Ad, and the post the account meant to sponsor stays unpromoted under that campaign.

You may want to confirm the objective supports Thought Leader Ads before building the ad set since the objective is chosen early and is not changed on an existing campaign. A few things are worth checking:
  • The campaign objective is one of the objectives that supports Thought Leader Ads, such as brand awareness, engagement, or video views
  • The ad format selected for the ad set is one the member post uses, such as single image, video, or event
  • The member's sponsorship permission has been requested and approved, which is what the ad set needs to run
Where the goal genuinely calls for an objective that does not support the format, you may want to consider a separate upper-funnel campaign for the Thought Leader Ad and keep the conversion goal on its own campaign.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business (B2B) store, the goal is often a conversion or a sale, which sits under an objective that may not support Thought Leader Ads. You may want to confirm the format is run under a supported upper-funnel objective such as brand awareness or engagement, rather than attached to the conversion campaign where it cannot launch.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-capture account, the instinct is to attach every ad to a lead generation objective, which does not support Thought Leader Ads. You may want to confirm the Thought Leader Ad runs under a supported objective such as engagement, and that the lead-capture goal stays on its own campaign.

learn_more.source_title: Create thought leader ads in Campaign Manager

investigate.intro: In Campaign Manager, confirm the objective on any campaign meant to carry a Thought Leader Ad, and that the format is actually selectable under that objective.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager, select the ad account, and open the campaign that is meant to carry the Thought Leader Ad.
Note the campaign objective and confirm it is one that supports Thought Leader Ads, such as brand awareness, engagement, or video views.
Open the ad set's creative step and confirm the member-post format is selectable. If the format does not appear, the objective or the ad format does not support it.
Confirm the member's sponsorship request has been approved. An approved post is required before the ad can run under any objective.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per campaign meant to carry a Thought Leader Ad, whether the objective supports the format and whether the format is selectable in the ad set. A campaign whose objective supports the format and lets the practitioner select it passes. A campaign built under an unsupported objective, where the format cannot be selected, is recorded as a fail with the campaign name and the objective at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the objective and whether the Thought Leader Ad format is selectable for each relevant campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that a campaign on {{site_name}} meant to carry a Thought Leader Ad is built under an objective the format does not support, so the member-post ad cannot launch under it. Thought Leader Ads are available only for a limited set of objectives, such as brand awareness, engagement, and video views, and the setup work does not produce a running ad when the objective falls outside that set. {{voice_recommend}} rebuilding the Thought Leader Ad under a supported objective, keeping any conversion or lead-capture goal on its own separate campaign, and confirming the member-post format is selectable in the ad set before the campaign goes live.
